title: cakile maritima subsp. latifolia
intro: Cakile maritima subsp. latifolia, also known as sea rocket, is a subspecies of the sea rocket plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is a biennial or annual plant that thrives in coastal areas and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and lower blood pressure. Traditionally, sea rocket has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
